title: Worth And The Worthy.
content:
If thou anything hast, let me have it, I'll pay what is proper;
    If thou anything art, let us our spirits exchange.
meaning:
Friedrich Schiller's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Worth And The Worthy."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
